DISCOVERY 612, APPEARANCES: JILL J. FIGG, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY FOR DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY. JODY LEWITTER, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF/OPPOSING PARTY. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS TO NOTEBOOK. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF MEDICAL RECORDS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS IT SEEKS THE PRODUCTION OF THE NOTEBOOK. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE NOTEBOOKS IS PRIVILEGED. THE COURT ORDERS THE COMPLETE MEDICAL RECORDS PRODUCED FOR IN CAMERA REVIEW. AFTER IN CAMERA REVIEW OF MEDICAL RECORDS, 4 ENTRIES ORDERED PRODUCED. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

DISCOVERY, 612, NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL APPEARANCE AT MEDICAL EXAMINATION IS DENIED AS DEFENDANT HAS NOT MADE A SHOWING OF GOOD CAUSE. DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHOWN WHY THE EXAMINATION AND REPORT ATTACHED TO PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION AS EXHIBIT A IN INSUFFICIENT. DEFENDANT HAS NOT OFFERED ANY EXPERT OPINION WHICH WOULD SUPPORT A FINDING THAT A NEW EXAMINATION IS NECESSARY. ADDITIONALLY, THERE IS NO SHOWING THAT THE REQUESTED EXAMINATION, REMOTE IN TIME FROM THE STATEMENTS THAT ARE THE SUBJECT OF THIS LITIGATION, WOULD BE RELEVANT. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

DISCOVERY 612, NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS FOR EDUCATIONAL RECORDS IS GRANTED. ANY SUBPOENA MUST BE LIMITED TO RECORDS REFERENCING ANY ASTHMATIC CONDITION. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE OVERBREADTH OF THE SUBPOENAS AND THEIR INVASION OF PLAINTIFF'S RIGHT TO PRIVACY WERE MISUSES OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. SANCTIONS ARE IMPOSED AGAINST DEFENDANT IN THE AMOUNT OF $947.70, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

DISCOVERY 612, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO MODIFY PROTECTIVE ORDER -- DARIA DIMITROFF APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY. JILL J. FIGG APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT CCSF, AARON SCHULTZ APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT TIMOTHY MILES. ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: MOTION IS DENIED. THE PARTIES AND COUNSEL MAY CONTINUE TO MEET AND CONFER TO REACH AN AGREEMENT, BUT THIS MOTION IS NOT SUFFICIENT TO ADDRESS ISSUES OF PRIVILEGE WHICH PLAINTIFF INCORRECTLY ASSERTS DO NOT EXIST. THE MOTION ALSO IS INSUFFICIENT TO ADDRESS ISSUES THAT MAY APPLY TO THE UNDEFINED AND INCOMPLETELY IDENTIFIED CLASS OF PERSONS SEEKING ACCESS TO THE DOCUMENTS. ADDITIONALLY, AS THE MOTION SEEKS TO MODIFY THE ORDER AS TO ALL DOCUMENTS, IT IS OVERLY BROAD. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
